facts.claim: Frontend stack. Legacy: Next.js ^12.2.2 pages-router + React ^17.0.2 + AntD ^4.21.7, LESS `modifyVars` theming; 463 .ts/.tsx, 153 .less (4,888 lines), 117 component dirs, 27 routed pages; PT-BR-only, zero i18n. Recommend: upgrade to Next.js app-router + React 18+ + AntD v5 `ConfigProvider theme.token/algorithm` (replaces Less modifyVars/dynamic-antd-theme); add real i18n, PT-BR sole initial locale; moment->dayjs. Notes: AntD v4->v5 breaking change across 281 .tsx files; pages->app-router touches all 27 pages + SSR auth (nookies); clinical form engine transfers at moderate cost.

Dark/compact base theme. Legacy: one compiled bundle, `getThemeVariables({dark:true,compact:true})` + 15 brand vars; no light/comfortable build sibling; primary #fe6d01. Recommend: keep dark+compact as explicit documented default (glare/density) but as one of two symmetric, runtime-switchable token-driven variants, not a sole hardcoded build. Notes: rationale never stated in code, inferred; pending ICU-user validation; 4-combo QA surface if independently toggleable.

Light mode. Legacy: 4-layer client overlay - cookie-gated (`theme.light`/nookies) pref, full `window.location.reload()` on toggle; dynamic-imported LightTheme.less; `@import antd/dist/antd.css` stock light + ~30 hand patches; 23/153 .less files hand-roll `&.light{}` blocks (1 drifts to `&.is-light`). Recommend: replace with token-driven, reload-free switching - CSS custom properties + `data-theme` attr + AntD v5 algorithm switching. Notes: rated the single most fragile legacy mechanism, 'must not be carried forward'; reload discards in-flight form state/drawers/scroll.

Per-tenant branding. Legacy: single hex `cor_primaria` via native color input; `changeColorTheme()` calls `dynamic-antd-theme` recompile AND sets `--primary-color`/`--primary-shadow-color`(alpha `2e`); applied TWICE per load (_app.tsx hardcodes #fe6d01 first, PageContainer overwrites later) = flash-of-default-orange bug; consumed at 60+ sites; only per-tenant customization that exists. Recommend: resolve one deterministic color token server-side/before first paint into AntD v5 `theme.token.colorPrimary`, applied once; drop dynamic-antd-theme. Notes: no contrast/validation guard on tenant hex today.

Token governance. Legacy: 15 Less variables in variables.less duplicated into next.config.js; variables.less NEVER @import-ed (dead copy, grep-confirmed). 5/15 tokens zero consumers: `@skeleton-text`(#eee3), `@degree`(120deg), `@grad-perc`(-100%), `@header-opacity`(0.8), `@border-width`(3px). Live bug: `var(--warning-color)` read by Display.tsx, never set anywhere -> warning treatment silently no-ops. Recommend: single tokens JSON/YAML source generating build config + CSS vars, with a lint asserting every `var(--x)` resolves. Notes: do NOT port the 5 dead tokens; MUST fix `--warning-color`.

No formal scales (spacing/radius/elevation/z-index/motion/type). Legacy: 0 tokens for these 6 dims vs 15 color tokens (~340:15 literal ratio). Spacing 100% literal px/rem. Radius: 13 literals, 3 clusters, duplicate px=rem pairs (8px=0.5rem, 10px=0.625rem, 12px=0.75rem). Elevation: 23 box-shadow decls, no token. Z-index: 11 literals (1 x10, 2 x4, 99999 x2, 1031 x2, 9999, 999). Motion: ad hoc 0.2s/0.3s/0.4s/0.6s/1s/150ms. Type: Poppins only, 20+ unscaled sizes, only 4 weights vs 500/600 loaded, no monospace. BUG: non-monotonic root font-size - 93.75% at <=1590px, 85.5% at <=1480px, 100% at <=1260px; cascade makes 100% win at <=1260px so 85.5% only applies in the 1261-1480px band. Recommend: full formal scales for all 6 dims from one source (8-pt spacing; 3-tier radius; elevation pair; named z-index/motion scales; bounded type scale w/ 400/500/600 + monospace for tabular clinical values). Notes: do NOT port the non-monotonic cascade - it's a bug.

Neumorphic elevation. Legacy: 23 box-shadow decls, flat vs neumorphic dual-shadow, zero shared token. `ItemDefault` dark `5px 5px 10px #0b0b0b,-5px -5px 10px #1d1d1d` drops to FLAT in light (`0px 0px 10px #d1d1d1`) - asymmetric. `ItemNotificacao` KEEPS dual-shadow both themes - symmetric. `MobileCardWrapper`(0 usages, dead) copy-pastes ItemDefault's dark values. Recommend: governed elevation-token scale (sm/md/lg x dark/light) via one shared utility, adopting the symmetric pattern as default; contrast check both themes for clinical content over embossed surfaces; honor prefers-contrast with flat-shadow fallback. Notes: distinctive validated signature worth preserving; soft-UI shadows costly at scale (bed-board grid) - must benchmark paint cost.

PageContainer app shell. Legacy: mounted at 25 call sites; independently REST-fetches empresa->estabelecimento->setor every mount via local useState, no shared cache; empresa uses useEffect keyed on ID, estabelecimento/setor use useMountEffect (2 mismatched mechanisms); render gated by JSX conditional `token && empresaAtual.id && !loading`. SSR `validateRoute` only checks token-cookie presence; `ignorePermission` defaults `true`(off), no page overrides it. Recommend: keep one shell, back tenant context with shared cache keyed on route IDs; move auth/tenant gate to a server-side route guard ahead of shell render; flip permission default to ON. Notes: today a stale-but-cookie-present token gets real markup streamed before client fetch fails.

Information architecture. Legacy: no persistent nav/AntD Menu; 5 uncoordinated wayfinding mechanisms - drill-down tiles; header context switcher (lateral movement); CircularMenu FAB (bed-board page only); hand-built Tag breadcrumb in PageContainer limited to estabelecimento/setor (never reaches leito/ocupacao - deepest screens have NO breadcrumb); permission-filtered settings tiles. A fully-built AntD Breadcrumb w/ UUID-aware parsing exists w/ ZERO call sites (dead, abandoned). Recommend: keep tile drill-down + header switcher; replace the ad hoc trail with ONE persistent reusable breadcrumb in the shared shell, extended to full depth. Notes: search/command-palette flagged as future scope, not this recommendation; sidebar deferred pending clinical validation.

Drawer-in-drawer pattern. Legacy: shared `DrawerBuilder` (width 95vw below 1260px collapseRule else 50vw, custom close X, Salvar/Fechar footer, destroyOnClose) at 16 call sites; nests 2 levels deep; ListOcupacoes independently stacks 4 separate DrawerBuilder instances, each own boolean + manual onClose cleanup; NO shared stack manager, no Esc/back coordination between nested drawers, no deliberate focus-trap beyond AntD v4 default. Recommend: keep the visual pattern/conventions; add a generic overlay-stack manager owning nesting depth, Esc/back (closes only topmost), per-level focus trapping. Notes: new infrastructure, no legacy equivalent; migrating 16 sites to a new API is real mechanical cost.

Responsive strategy. Legacy: `collapseRule=1260`(px), `collapseRuleMobile=800`(px) via useWindowSize in 49 files (42 ref collapseRule). 3 sub-strategies: class-toggling ~42 files; forked trees (1 instance: GridView/MobileView); ad hoc bucket if-chains - ListOcupacoes's 5-bucket chain (w>2800->4; 1800<w<2400->6; 1260<w<1800->8; 800<w<1260->12; w<800->24) has NO rule for band (2400,2800] -> span undefined -> full-width cards bug on large monitors; ListDashboard reimplements a DIFFERENT drifted bucket set (w>1800->6; 1260<w<1800->8; 800<w<1260->12; w<800->24, no >2800 tier). Duplicate Less literal `@collapse-rule:1260px`. 33 one-off @media widths (324-1590px). Recommend: single named breakpoint set generating JS+CSS; keep the fork hatch narrowly; replace both bucket chains with ONE shared unit-tested density function covering full width domain; retire the 33 widths. Notes: validate against >2800px monitors, legacy silently mishandled.

Canonical primitives. Legacy: 117 component dirs, 281 .tsx, 126 .less, no authoring contract. Well-adopted: DrawerBuilder(16 sites), AlertDelete, Ball status-dot, MaterialIcon. BUT: 2nd untyped bespoke SVG icon system, no shared sizing contract; 2 tab implementations (AntD Tabs vs bespoke CustomTabs, only 2 files); 2 unread-count badge treatments (AntD Badge vs hand-rolled div capped '+99', colored from @secondary-color not the alert palette); 3 fully-built components never imported (Breadcrumbs w/ UUID parsing, MobileCardWrapper, SideFixedButton). Recommend: canonicalize the 4 good primitives; consolidate tabs/badges into one impl each; retire the 3 dead components; CI blocks a second implementation of a registered primitive. Notes: optionally adopt a headless library for generic non-clinical concerns.

Clinical severity colors (statusTrilha). Legacy: `statusTrilha.ts` typed `as any`, 5 states (NEUTRO/VERMELHO/AMARELO/LARANJA/ASSISTIDO) x 6 shades each, e.g. VERMELHO.ballColor=#FF1633, AMARELO.ballColor=#ffd900; ASSISTIDO always overrides raw alert when assistido===true; consumed via inline style at 7 components, not a theme provider; correctly decoupled from --primary-color. Reinvented w/ divergent hex in 6+ locations, incl. occupancy-gauge near-miss amber #FFAB00 vs statusTrilha.AMARELO #ffd900. AntD semantic tokens (@success/@warning/@danger/@info/@default) have ZERO call sites - dead. TWO LIVE BUGS: (1) severity-blind toast - DisplayNotificaoes hardcodes icon color #FFAB00 regardless of actual severity; (2) hardcoded-VERMELHO criteria panel - TabRecomendacoes colors by literal 'VERMELHO' instead of the criterion's actual alerta. Recommend: promote to a first-class, typed, contrast-checked `clinical.*` scale, consumed only via a shared primitive, structurally separate from `brand.*`; lint/CI blocks raw severity hex outside the token module. Notes: HIGHEST-PRIORITY clinical-design decision (patient safety); MUST fix both live bugs, not port; new API must model ASSISTIDO's override.

No abnormal-value flagging. Legacy: zero value-driven visual encoding anywhere - vitals (15 fields incl. HR, RR, BP, temperature, SpO2, HGT, ventilation mode, O2 flow, FiO2, consciousness, pain) render via one neutral ListItem row (SpO2 99% vs 60% identical); assistive labs (incl. RASS, Glasgow, delirium, fluid balance, leukocytes, platelets, CRP, lactate, pH, bicarbonate, pO2, pCO2, P/F ratio, creatinine, urea, bilirubin) likewise unencoded; fluid-balance grid gives every non-zero cell a fixed green pill `#42e26f` regardless of magnitude; SOFA score always static `Tag color="warning"` whether 0 or 20+. `RenderTagTable` (exactly the needed primitive) called with literal `"var(--primary-color)"` at all 8 sites, ALL non-clinical admin tables. ONLY numeric-to-color mapping anywhere: bed-occupancy gauge (>70% red, >50% amber, else green) - operational, not clinical. Recommend: centralized reference-range service + shared severity scale (normal/mild/moderate/critical) for vitals/labs/fluid-balance/SOFA; extend RenderTagTable; design to ALSO accept agent-derived signals. Notes: audit's TOP clinical UX gap; ranges need age/context adjustment and clinical sign-off.

Clinical form engine. Legacy: `FormDadosProntuario` takes a config array (not markup); `SelectCampoType` dispatches 10 field types (string/select/interval/number/boolean/checkbox/data/list/masked/multicheck) to 10 typed renderers. 14 role-specific configs drive it (nursing, physio, speech, physician, pharmacy, music therapy, nutrition, psychology, tech-nursing, admission, movement, remove-patient, intercurrence, fluid balance). Configs are loosely/`as any`-shaped despite declared type. No Steps wizard; multi-step simulated via 3 uncoordinated mechanisms (accordion, tabs, sequential drawers). Conditional fields: 3 SEPARATE mechanisms - group nullify Switch (nulls submitted value only, fields stay mounted - data-integrity gap), sub-group checavel Switch (display:none, still mounted), permission/tenant-type disabling. SOFA display static regardless of score. Recommend: preserve+modernize - typed config schema (shared w/ backend/FHIR layer) + ONE declarative visibility/nullability rule engine; defer Steps-wizard pending workflow validation. Notes: 'strongest, most valuable IP in the legacy frontend'; 14 configs need re-authoring (real migration cost).

Feedback/loading patterns. Legacy: `handleApiError` called individually in 86 catch blocks across 39 files (not an interceptor) - always identical AntD `Modal.error`; validation/403/500 all get an identical amber Tag regardless of severity; assumes DRF-shaped error body. `SkeletonList` renders generic rows (default 4, itemHeight 40px), not content-shaped. `FadeLoading` is a full-viewport blocking spinner used for 'most mutations,' no documented skeleton-vs-spinner rule. Recommend: centralize error handling in an HTTP-client interceptor, classify by explicit severity (validation|permission|server) independent of backend shape, map to visual weight (inline/toast/modal); formalize loading rule - content-shaped skeletons for page loads, non-blocking inline spinners for mutations by default, full-screen block reserved for rare destructive cases. Notes: AI-agent context needs 'needs attention now' vs 'still processing' distinguished without blocking the screen.

Fragmented real-time. Legacy: 3 independent mechanisms, NO shared reconnect/backoff. (1) Notifications: raw `w3cwebsocket`, TWO connections per user (list + popover, debounced 2s); read-marking sent back over the SAME socket; `ws.onerror` only logs, no reconnect. (2) Chat/presence: Firestore `onSnapshot` on ONE document shape `chats/{setorId}/usuarios/{usuarioId}`, used only to trigger REST refetch - Firestore never becomes system of record (sound principle). (3) Bed board/dashboard: REST polling via `setInterval(reloadTime seconds)` from per-tenant `empresa.tempo_atualizacao`, gated by a manual toggle, no staleness indicator; NO push channel here. Net: same alert reaches the bell instantly via WebSocket but the grid only next poll tick (up to `tempo_atualizacao`s later) - bell/grid can visibly disagree. Recommend: ONE push transport (WebSocket/SSE) with shared reconnect/backoff, topic multiplexing for all 3 use cases; preserve the Firestore-boundary principle - realtime is signal/transport only, REST/DB remains system of record. Notes: LIFE-SAFETY risk - the most safety-critical view is currently the least live.

Client integration/authorization. Legacy: `validateRoute(ctx)` reads token/permission cookies via nookies, redirects only if NO token present; `ignorePermission` DEFAULTS `true`(off), zero pages override it - SSR enforces 'logged in,' never 'authorized for this page.' Real gating entirely client-side via `useEffectivePermissions()`. `AuthProvider` is a single global Context with NO query cache (no react-query/SWR), re-verifies token every mount, 30-day cookie. File uploads base64-encode client-side then POST (~33% payload inflation), not signed direct-to-storage. ICU bed camera is a bare `<iframe>` to a microservice; its 'privacy' blur(20px) toggle is a VISUAL MASK ONLY - stream keeps running, never paused. Recommend: deny-by-default route guard requiring explicit per-route permission declaration; client query-cache layer; base64 kept for small attachments, signed direct-to-storage for large media; camera wrapped in a first-party player shell with a privacy toggle that actually gates the feed. Notes: fixes client-side assumptions only - REST API must independently enforce authorization; client guard is defense-in-depth only.

constraints.text: Tenant primary-color token MUST be resolved once, server-side or before first paint, with no client-side re-application step (eliminates the legacy flash-of-default-color race).
Every `var(--x)` referenced in source MUST resolve to a generated token definition; CI/lint MUST fail the build otherwise (closes the `--warning-color` silent no-op bug).
The responsive root type/font scale MUST be monotonic across breakpoints; the legacy's non-monotonic 93.75%/85.5%/100% cascade MUST NOT be ported forward.
Any surface carrying clinical text/status content over a neumorphic/embossed background MUST pass an explicit WCAG contrast check in both light and dark themes before shipping.

Route-level permission enforcement MUST default to on (deny-by-default), inverting legacy's `ignorePermission=true`; the auth/tenant gate MUST run server-side ahead of shell render, not a client JSX conditional.
No second, competing implementation of a registered canonical UI primitive (breadcrumb, tabs, icon system, count badge, drawer shell) may ship; CI MUST block a duplicate of a registered concern.
The shared responsive density/column-span function MUST cover the full viewport-width domain with no gaps - the legacy dead band (2400px,2800px] where span was undefined MUST NOT recur.
Clinical severity color tokens (`clinical.*`) MUST remain structurally separate from tenant brand tokens (`brand.*`); tenant rebranding MUST NOT alter what a clinical severity color means.

MUST fix, not port, the two live severity-color bugs: (a) toast icon color MUST derive from the alert's actual severity, never hardcoded; (b) criteria panels MUST color by each criterion's own alert value, never a hardcoded severity key.
Clinical form field configs MUST be validated against a strongly-typed schema (not `as any`) so a malformed config is caught at compile/build time, not at runtime.
Centralized error-severity classification (validation | permission | server) MUST NOT assume any particular backend error-body shape (e.g. must not be DRF-specific).

The realtime transport layer MUST NOT become a second source of truth; every realtime payload MUST either trigger a refetch or carry a thin, idempotent patch reconciled against the authoritative record.
Notification alerts and bed-board/dashboard alert state MUST be delivered via the same push channel/latency class so they cannot visibly disagree about the same event.
The backend REST API MUST independently enforce authorization on every request regardless of client-side guard state; the client guard is defense-in-depth/UX only, never the source of truth.
Any 'privacy' UI toggle (e.g. ICU bed camera blur) MUST actually gate/stop the underlying feed, not merely apply a client-side visual mask over a stream that keeps running.

open_questions: ADR-0002/0003: final token architecture; whether all 4 dark/light x compact/comfortable combos ship - pending ratification.
ADR-0006: definitive scale values for spacing/radius/z-index/motion/type - pending a design pass.
ADR-0007: sm/md/lg elevation values and paint-cost budget on dense bed-board screens - unbenchmarked.
ADR-0009: whether a persistent sidebar should be revisited with real clinical wayfinding data.
ADR-0010: overlay-stack manager implementation choice; which flows warrant a routed page instead.
ADR-0011: final breakpoint values; which call sites qualify for the JS-fork exception vs CSS reflow.
ADR-0013: whether re-derived severity hex may differ from legacy ('red bed = crisis' muscle memory) - needs clinical sign-off.
ADR-0014: severity-band count (3-tier vs 4-tier) and age/context-adjusted reference ranges - pending clinical ratification.
ADR-0015: config-schema technology choice; whether a Steps-wizard is wanted.
ADR-0017: concrete realtime transport (self-hosted WebSocket/SSE vs managed pub/sub) - depends on backend/hosting decision.
ADR-0018: query-cache library and storage-signing approach; whether backend authorization is actually enforced per-endpoint requires separate backend verification.
